summary: Implements the refresh.Fetcher interface against the gotd/td Telegram MTProto client — resolves usernames, fetches message history, extracts text messages

requires: Caller provides a connected *tg.Client (session already established)
username may include or omit leading '@'
guarantees: Returns only messages with non-empty text (tg.Message.Message field); service messages, media-only messages, and deleted placeholders are filtered out
When afterMsgID > 0, sets MinID so only newer messages are returned

state: Holds reference to *tg.Client; all state lives in the client — TelegramFetcher itself is stateless

observations.text: Username resolution iterates resolved.Chats and takes the first *tg.Channel — if a username resolves to multiple entities (e.g., a linked group), the first channel in the response wins

extractMessages handles three MessagesMessagesClass variants (MessagesMessages, MessagesMessagesSlice, MessagesChannelMessages) but returns an error for any unexpected type rather than silently returning empty
Directly depends on gotd/td *tg.Client — no interface indirection between TelegramFetcher and the network layer; testability requires the Fetcher interface defined in refresh.go
No retry or rate-limit handling; transient Telegram API errors propagate immediately to the caller as fetch errors accumulated in RefreshResult.Errors
